Height: 13 m (rear mound) |built = Mid-5th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}})
(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label=[[:en:Danjōzan Kofun|Danbayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label=[[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|qq=|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|qq=|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}}

(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label=[[:en:Danjōzan Kofun|Danbayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label=[[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label=[[:en:Danjōzan Kofun|Danbayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label=[[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label=[[:en:Danjōzan Kofun|Danbayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label=[[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Height: 13 m (rear mound) |built = Mid-5th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}})
(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label=[[:en:Danjōzan Kofun|Danbayama Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label=[[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Kaihō Tennō]]. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Height: 13 m (rear mound) |built = Mid-5th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}})
(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label={{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|en|Danjōzan Kofun|WD=Q22119379|lt=Danbayama Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label={{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaihō Tennō}}. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

(Possibly another burial chamber in the rear mound) |Interred_individual = Presumed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}: {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}} |burial goods = Swords, spears, armor, arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Imperial designation1 = Designated as an "Imperial {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}" (Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) |notes = Second-largest kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture, largest in the Tanba region |map = {{location map+|Japan Hyōgo|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=5|coordinates=12.86|lon_deg=135|lon_min=18|lon_sec=16.03|map_dot_label='''Kumobe
Kurumazuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label={{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|en|Danjōzan Kofun|WD=Q22119379|lt=Danbayama Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label={{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba no Michinushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaihō Tennō}}. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The mound is keyhole-shaped ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaihō Tennō|1=Emperor Kaika}}. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label={{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|en|Danjōzan Kofun|WD=Q22119379|lt=Danbayama Kofun|1=Danjōzan Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label={{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpokoenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaihō Tennō|1=Emperor Kaika}}. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpōkōenfun.svg|marksize=23}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=49|coordinates=20.76|lon_deg=134|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.57|map_dot_label={{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|en|Danjōzan Kofun|qid=Q22119379|lt=Danbayama Kofun|1=Danjōzan Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpōkōenfun.svg|marksize=22}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=34|coordinates=37|coordinates=46.29|lon_deg=135|lon_min=2|lon_sec=44.89|map_dot_label={{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|qid=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpōkōenfun.svg|marksize=26}} {{location map~|Japan Hyōgo|coordinates=35|coordinates=20|coordinates=26.53|lon_deg=134|lon_min=50|lon_sec=35.26|map_dot_label={{ill|Ikeda Kofun|ja|池田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Kofun|1=Ikeda Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043080}}|map_dot_label_size=100|position=top|mark=Zenpōkōenfun.svg|marksize=22}} }}|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} {{coordinates List}} '''Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun''' (雲部車塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]], Hyōgo Prefecture. The mound is keyhole-shaped ({{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}). The identity of the interred individual remains unknown, but the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as the "Kumobe {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}," associating it with {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba-no-Michinushi no Mikoto|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368587}}, a grandson of the 9th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Kaihō Tennō|1=Emperor Kaika}}. With its size Ranked first in the Tanba region and second in Hyōgo Prefecture, the kofun dates to the mid-5th century (mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}). == Overview == [[File:751121 Kmb Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}}]] Situated in eastern [[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba Sasayama]] along the Tanba basin's edge where it connects with the {{ill|Kameoka basin|ja|亀岡盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka basin|1=Kameoka Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11181997}}, this keyhole-shaped kofun stands as a strategic and significant site in the region. ... {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|雲部車塚古墳|version=101931643|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雲部車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kumobe Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215205}} [[Category:History of Tanbasasayama, Hyogo]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]
